tasks.title: Write failing regression test for OG image PNG output (RED)
tasks.description:
Write failing Vitest test FIRST (RED), then T-002 makes it GREEN.

Create test file: `apps/web/src/pages/api/__tests__/og.test.ts`

Test must:
- Mock the Astro request/response cycle to call the handler in `apps/web/src/pages/api/og.ts`
- Assert `Content-Type` header is `image/png` (currently returns `image/svg+xml` — test MUST fail)
- Assert the response body is non-empty binary (Buffer/Uint8Array)

Acceptance:
- `pnpm test` runs the test file and it fails (Content-Type mismatch: svg vs png)
- Test covers the happy-path GET with any valid query parameters
- Use Vitest `it` with descriptive label: 'GET /api/og returns Content-Type: image/png'

subtasks.title: Create test file skeleton with Vitest imports
Mock Astro GET handler invocation
Assert Content-Type: image/png (RED — will fail)
Assert non-empty binary response body

tasks.title: Fix OG image endpoint to return PNG (1200x630)
tasks.description:
Fix `apps/web/src/pages/api/og.ts` so it renders and returns a valid PNG image.

Affected file: `apps/web/src/pages/api/og.ts` (lines 15, 45)

Changes required:
1. Change `Content-Type` response header from `image/svg+xml` to `image/png`
2. Rasterize the SVG output to PNG using `resvg-js` (preferred — no new infra) or `satori` + `@resvg/resvg-js`. Add the chosen dependency to `apps/web/package.json`.
3. Update the JSDoc at line 15 to accurately state PNG output, 1200x630px
4. Ensure the response body is a valid PNG binary (not SVG text)

Implementation guidance: use `resvg-js` to convert the existing SVG template to PNG — `const resvg = new Resvg(svgString, { width: 1200, height: 630 }); return resvg.render().asPng()`

Acceptance:
- T-001 test passes (GREEN)
- Manual: curl the endpoint, pipe to `file -` → reports PNG
- Image dimensions 1200x630 pixels

subtasks.title: Add resvg-js (or equivalent) to apps/web/package.json
Import rasterization library in og.ts
Rasterize SVG output to PNG buffer
Set Content-Type: image/png on response
Fix JSDoc at line 15 to state PNG output
Verify T-001 test is GREEN

tasks.title: Add preconnect resource hints to BaseLayout.astro
tasks.description:
Add `<link rel="preconnect">` hints to `BaseLayout.astro` for Cloudinary and the API origin.

Changes required:
1. Add `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://res.cloudinary.com">` in `<head>`
2. Add `<link rel="preconnect" href="{API_URL}">` using the `HOSPEDA_API_URL` env var (already validated in `apps/web/src/utils/env.ts`) — not hardcoded
3. Optionally add a `lcpImageUrl` prop to `BaseLayout` props so pages that know their LCP image can emit `<link rel="preload" as="image" href="{lcpImageUrl}">` — document this pattern even if not wired to all pages immediately

Acceptance:
- Any page rendered by BaseLayout has `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://res.cloudinary.com">` in `<head>`
- Any page rendered by BaseLayout has `<link rel="preconnect">` for the API origin with env-derived value
- No hardcoded production domain in resource hints

tasks.title: Install @astrojs/rss and create blog RSS feed endpoint
tasks.description:
Install `@astrojs/rss` and create the blog RSS feed.

Affected files:
- `apps/web/package.json` (add `@astrojs/rss` dependency)
- New: `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/publicaciones/rss.xml.ts`

Changes required:
1. Add `@astrojs/rss` to `apps/web/package.json` and run `pnpm install`
2. Create `rss.xml.ts` under `[lang]/publicaciones/` that fetches blog posts and generates RSS 2.0 feed
3. Feed must include per-item: `title`, `description` (excerpt), `link` (absolute URL with locale prefix), `pubDate`
4. Feed title and description must be i18n-aware using `@repo/i18n`
5. Feed URL must be locale-prefixed: `/es/publicaciones/rss.xml`, `/en/publicaciones/rss.xml`, `/pt/publicaciones/rss.xml`

Acceptance:
- GET `/es/publicaciones/rss.xml` returns `Content-Type: application/xml` or `application/rss+xml`
- Response body is valid RSS 2.0
- Feed contains items with title, link, and pubDate

tasks.title: Resolve D-1: Wire or delete JSON-LD components on all detail pages
tasks.description:
⚠ PARTIALLY BLOCKED ON USER DECISION D-1 — the TLD fix (T-013) is independent and unblocked.

Once D-1 is resolved:

**If ADOPT (recommended):**
Affected files: `apps/web/src/components/seo/LodgingBusinessJsonLd.astro`, `EventJsonLd.astro`, `PlaceJsonLd.astro`, all detail pages emitting inline JSON-LD, `apps/web/CLAUDE.md`
1. Audit all detail pages that hand-roll JSON-LD inline: accommodation detail (`[lang]/alojamientos/[slug].astro`), blog post detail, event detail, place detail
2. Replace inline JSON-LD on each page with the canonical component import
3. Remove duplicate inline JSON-LD blocks
4. Update `apps/web/CLAUDE.md` to document the component as the canonical path

**If DELETE:**
1. Remove `LodgingBusinessJsonLd.astro`, `EventJsonLd.astro`, `PlaceJsonLd.astro` (keep `ArticleJsonLd.astro` after TLD fix, or remove it too)
2. Update `apps/web/CLAUDE.md` to document inline JSON-LD as the pattern

Acceptance (adopt):
- Each detail page type has exactly one JSON-LD block emitted by the canonical component
- No inline duplicate JSON-LD blocks remain on any detail page

Acceptance (delete):
- No orphaned component files in `apps/web/src/components/seo/`
- CLAUDE.md documents the current pattern

subtasks.title: Await D-1 decision from user (adopt vs delete)
Audit all detail pages emitting inline JSON-LD
(Adopt) Wire canonical components on each detail page
(Adopt) Remove inline duplicate JSON-LD blocks
(Delete) Remove unused component files
Update apps/web/CLAUDE.md with current pattern

4. `twitter:image:alt` meta tag must be present with non-empty value (currently missing — RED)

tasks.title: Fix SEOHead.astro: absolute URL coercion + add og:image dimension and alt meta
tasks.description:
Fix `apps/web/src/components/seo/SEOHead.astro` to coerce og:image to absolute URL and add missing meta tags.

Affected file: `apps/web/src/components/seo/SEOHead.astro` (line 63)

Changes required:
1. At line 63, coerce `image` prop to absolute URL: `const absoluteImage = image ? new URL(image, Astro.site ?? siteBase).href : undefined`
2. Use `absoluteImage` in the `og:image` meta tag (replace raw `image` prop usage)
3. Add `<meta property="og:image:width" content="1200" />` when image is present
4. Add `<meta property="og:image:height" content="630" />` when image is present
5. Add `<meta property="og:image:alt" content="{imageAlt}" />` — add `imageAlt` prop (default to `title`)
6. Add `<meta name="twitter:image:alt" content="{imageAlt}" />`

Acceptance:
- T-015 tests all pass (GREEN)
- No page with relative og:image path will emit a relative URL
- All image-bearing pages get og:image:width, og:image:height, og:image:alt, twitter:image:alt

subtasks.title: Add absolute URL coercion for image prop at line 63
Add og:image:width and og:image:height meta tags

tasks.title: Add eager prop to AccommodationCard.astro for above-fold images
tasks.description:
Add an `eager` boolean prop to `AccommodationCard.astro` and wire it to listing pages.

Affected files:
- `apps/web/src/components/.../AccommodationCard.astro` (line 79)
- Listing/featured grid pages that render the first row of cards

Changes required:
1. Add optional `eager: boolean = false` prop to `AccommodationCard.astro`
2. When `eager` is true: render image with `loading="eager" fetchpriority="high"`
3. When `eager` is false (default): keep `loading="lazy"`
4. On the main accommodations listing page (`[lang]/alojamientos/index.astro`) and homepage featured grid: pass `eager={true}` to the first N cards (implementation decision: first 3 or a prop from parent)

Acceptance:
- AccommodationCard with `eager=true` renders `loading="eager"` and `fetchpriority="high"` on the image
- AccommodationCard with no `eager` prop (default) retains `loading="lazy"`
- Above-fold card rows on listing pages use `eager=true`

tasks.title: Remove body opacity:0 font gate from BaseLayout.astro
tasks.description:
Remove the body opacity transition used as a font loading gate from `BaseLayout.astro`.

Affected file: `apps/web/src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro` (lines 103-118)

Changes required:
1. Remove the CSS rule that sets `body { opacity: 0 }` on initial load
2. Remove the JavaScript/script tag that manages transitioning body opacity to 1 after font load
3. Verify `font-display: swap` is declared on web font `@font-face` rules (it should already be — this is the correct SEO-safe font strategy)
4. Test in dev with network throttled — if a problematic visual flash is found, document it with a comment and propose a scoped fix (do NOT re-add the full-page gate)

Acceptance:
- BaseLayout.astro has no CSS rule setting body opacity to 0 on initial load
- BaseLayout.astro has no script tag managing a body opacity transition for font loading
- Page body is visible immediately on cold load (no JS-managed delay to first paint)

subtasks.title: Remove CSS body opacity:0 rule (lines 103-118)
Remove font-loading opacity transition script
Confirm font-display: swap on @font-face rules
Test with throttled network; document any flash if observed

tasks.title: Change locale redirect from 302 to 301 in middleware
tasks.description:
Change the locale redirect status code from 302 (temporary) to 301 (permanent) in both middleware files.

Affected files:
- `apps/web/src/middleware.ts` (line 147)
- `apps/web/src/lib/middleware-helpers.ts` (line 301)

Changes required:
1. At `middleware.ts:147`, change the redirect status from `302` to `301`
2. At `middleware-helpers.ts:301`, change the redirect status from `302` to `301`
3. Verify no other redirect calls in these files use 302 for the locale redirect pattern

⚠ This change is irreversible in practice (browsers cache 301s). Confirm URL strategy is stable before shipping.

Acceptance:
- T-030 tests pass (GREEN): locale redirect returns 301
- GET `/alojamientos/` → 301 to `/es/alojamientos/`
- GET `/` → 301 to `/es/` (or default locale prefix)
- No other locale redirect in the codebase still uses 302
